title: Coronation Chicken Salad

ingredients.name: thick curd (hung 15 minutes to reduce whey), as a mayo substitute

subs.name: equal parts hung curd and mayonnaise for a richer, more traditional dressing
subs.notes: the original recipe used mayo only; curd is the Indian substitute

ingredients.name: ripe mango, peeled and finely diced
visual_checks: Alphonso or any sweet variety; or use 3 tbsp mango chutney
ingredients.notes: Alphonso or any sweet variety; or use 3 tbsp mango chutney

subs.name: tinned peach slices (drained and diced) or fresh apple — both provide the sweet counterpoint
subs.notes: if using mango chutney, reduce the sugar to zero

subs.name: cashews (toasted) or sunflower seeds for a nut-free version
subs.notes: cashews are widely available and add a similar crunch

ingredients.name: celery stalks, thinly sliced
visual_checks: adds crunch; substitute cucumber if unavailable
ingredients.notes: adds crunch; substitute cucumber if unavailable

subs.name: cucumber (deseeded and diced) or spring onion — both add crunch without the assertive celery flavour
subs.notes: cucumber releases water, so add just before serving

ingredients.name: sugar
visual_checks: balances the tang of the curd
ingredients.notes: balances the tang of the curd

steps.text: Place the chicken in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Add a pinch of salt. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a bare simmer and poach for 15–18 minutes until the chicken is cooked through with no pink in the centre — a thermometer should read 75°C at the thickest point. Lift out and cool for 10 minutes, then shred or cut into 2 cm pieces.

While the chicken cools, toast the flaked almonds in a dry frying pan over medium heat for 2–3 minutes, shaking occasionally, until golden and fragrant. Transfer immediately to a plate — they burn quickly once they start to colour.

Make the dressing: whisk together the hung curd, curry powder, lemon juice, sugar if using, salt and black pepper in a large bowl until smooth and creamy. Taste — the dressing should be gently spiced, slightly sweet and tangy.

Add the cooled chicken, diced mango, raisins and sliced celery to the dressing. Fold together gently until everything is evenly coated. If the dressing seems thick, stir in 1–2 tablespoons of cold water.

Scatter the toasted almonds and fresh coriander over the top just before serving. Coronation chicken is best served slightly chilled or at room temperature — not straight from the fridge. Serve in bread rolls, on buttered toast, over steamed basmati rice, or on a bed of soft lettuce leaves.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → equal parts hung curd and mayonnaise for a richer, more traditional dressing (the original recipe used mayo only; curd is the Indian substitute); mango → tinned peach slices (drained and diced) or fresh apple — both provide the sweet counterpoint (if using mango chutney, reduce the sugar to zero); almond → cashews (toasted) or sunflower seeds for a nut-free version (cashews are widely available and add a similar crunch); celery → cucumber (deseeded and diced) or spring onion — both add crunch without the assertive celery flavour (cucumber releases water, so add just before serving)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Thick hung curd is the natural substitute for the mayonnaise in the original recipe — hang regular dahi in a muslin cloth for 15 minutes to reduce the water. Alphonso or Kesar mango is exceptional in this dish when in season; raw mango chutney (sweetened) works well out of season. The mild curry powder can be made at home with equal parts cumin, coriander, turmeric and a pinch of garam masala. Serve over jeera rice for a complete meal.
